name: Homeostatic Reinforcement Learning with Drive-Reduction Reward
description: A value-based temporal-difference reinforcement learning agent where the reward signal is defined as drive reduction — the decrease in homeostatic displacement caused by an action. The drive D(h) is a power-law function of the distance between the agent's current energy level and a fixed homeostatic setpoint h*, formally equivalent to interoceptive surprise -ln p(h). The agent maintains a Q-table mapping discretized states (distance-to-nearest-food bin × energy bin) to action values, updated via TD(0). This implements the Keramati & Gutkin (2014) framework showing that maximizing discounted drive-reduction reward is mathematically dual to minimizing discounted homeostatic deviation, establishing 'physiological rationality'. An urgency mechanism increases exploitation when energy is critically low.

variables.name: physiological_state
variables.description: Current energy level of the agent, normalized to [0,1]. Internally tracked: increases when eating, decreases each step by metabolic cost.

variables.name: homeostatic_setpoint
variables.description: Ideal energy level the agent strives to maintain. Fixed throughout the simulation.

variables.name: drive
variables.description: Scalar homeostatic displacement: distance from setpoint raised to power n, scaled by m. Equivalent to surprise -ln p(h_t) under a Gaussian prior centered at h*.

variables.name: primary_reward
variables.description: Drive reduction achieved by the last action: r(t) = D(h(t)) - D(h(t+1)). Positive when action moves agent closer to setpoint.

variables.name: action_value_table
variables.description: Q-table mapping (state_index, action_index) to expected discounted sum of future drive reductions. State is discretized as (distance_to_nearest_food_bin, energy_bin).

variables.name: state_representation
variables.description: Discretized state tuple: (distance_to_nearest_food_bin, energy_bin). 5 distance bins × 5 energy bins = 25 states.

parameters.name: homeostatic_setpoint
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014): high but sub-maximal setpoint reflecting optimal physiology

parameters.name: drive_sensitivity
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014): multiplicative constant in drive function

parameters.name: drive_exponent
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014): quadratic drive approximates Euclidean distance in homeostatic space

parameters.name: temporal_discount_factor
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014): must be strictly < 1 for physiological rationality (P5)

parameters.name: learning_rate_td
parameters.source: Standard RL convention for TD(0) step size

parameters.name: action_inverse_temperature
parameters.source: Controls exploration-exploitation tradeoff; moderate exploitation bias

parameters.name: urgency_inverse_temperature
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014) / P5: temporal discounting forces rapid correction at low energy

parameters.name: critical_energy_threshold
parameters.source: Threshold below which urgency mode activates (reduced exploration)

parameters.name: metabolic_cost_move
parameters.source: Normalized energy cost per movement step

parameters.name: energy_gain_from_eating
parameters.source: Normalized resource gain; scaled by food palatability from env_spec

rules.description: Physiological state dynamics: energy changes based on action outcome. Eating increases energy (scaled by food palatability), movement and staying decrease energy by metabolic costs.
Drive function: homeostatic displacement equivalent to surprise. Quadratic in deviation from setpoint.
Primary reward as drive reduction: positive when action moves the agent closer to the setpoint.
rules.pseudocode:
D_old = m * abs(h_star - h_t_old) ** n
D_new = m * abs(h_star - h_t_new) ** n
r_t = D_old - D_new

rules.description: State discretization: continuous state is binned into a discrete tuple for Q-table indexing.
rules.pseudocode:
dist_to_nearest = min(manhattan_distance(pos, food_pos) for food_pos in food_list) if food_list else grid_width + grid_height
dist_bin = digitize(dist_to_nearest, [0, 1, 3, 5, 8])  # bins: at(0), near(1-2), med(3-4), far(5-7), vfar(8+)
energy_bin = int(clip(h_t * n_energy_bins, 0, n_energy_bins - 1))
s_t = (dist_bin, energy_bin)

rules.description: TD(0) Q-value update: temporal-difference learning with drive-reduction reward.
rules.pseudocode:
td_target = r_t + gamma * max(Q[s_new, a_prime] for a_prime in available_actions)
td_error = td_target - Q[s_old, a_old]
Q[s_old, a_old] = Q[s_old, a_old] + alpha * td_error

rules.description: Softmax action selection over Q-values, with urgency-modulated temperature when energy is critically low.
rules.pseudocode:
effective_beta = beta_urgent if h_t < h_critical else beta
logits = [effective_beta * Q[s_t, a] for a in available_actions]
# Mask eat if no food at current position: set logit to -inf
if not food_at_current_position:
    logits[eat_index] = -inf
probs = softmax(logits)
selected_action = sample_from(probs, available_actions)

decision_logic.description: Each step: (1) Read perception to get position and food locations. (2) Discretize state into (distance_bin, energy_bin) via R4. (3) Look up Q-values for all actions in current state. (4) Select action via softmax with urgency modulation (R6). After action execution in update(): (5) Update energy based on action result (R1). (6) Compute drive before and after (R2), then reward (R3). (7) Discretize new state (R4). (8) Update Q-table via TD(0) (R5).
pseudocode: food_at_pos = any(f['x'] == position[0] and f['y'] == position[1] for f in food_list)
dist_to_nearest = min(abs(f['x']-position[0]) + abs(f['y']-position[1]) for f in food_list)
effective_beta = beta_urgent if h_t < h_critical else beta
logits = {a: effective_beta * Q.get((s_t, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions}
store s_old = s_t, h_old = h_t for update()
td_target = r_t + gamma * max(Q.get((s_new, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions)
Q[(s_old, action)] = Q.get((s_old, action), 0.0) + alpha * td_error

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_at_current_position: any(f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y for f in perception['resources']['food'])
perception_to_variables.food_palatability: food['palatability'] for consumed food item (from env_spec: range [0.1, 1.0])
perception_to_variables.last_action_result: perception['last_action_result'] — used to detect successful eat

env_mapping.reward_source: Internal drive-reduction reward r(t) = D(h_old) - D(h_new); NOT the env reward. Env reward of 1.0 from eat is ignored; the agent's motivational signal is purely homeostatic drive reduction.

expected_behaviors.description: Drive increases monotonically as energy drops below the setpoint h*=0.8.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run agent without food for 20 steps. Record D_t each step. Assert D_t is monotonically increasing.

expected_behaviors.description: Eating produces positive reward (drive reduction) when energy is below setpoint.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h_t=0.3, execute eat with k_eat=0.3. Assert r_t > 0 (D decreased).

expected_behaviors.description: Q-values for 'eat' at distance_bin=0 (at food) converge to high values over episodes.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run 200 steps with food available. Assert Q[(0, low_energy_bin), 'eat'] > Q[(0, low_energy_bin), 'stay'].

expected_behaviors.description: Urgency mode: when energy < 0.2, agent exploits more aggressively (less random exploration).
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run agent with h_t=0.15. Record action entropy. Assert entropy is lower than when h_t=0.5.

expected_behaviors.description: Agent learns to navigate toward food: Q-values for movement toward food are higher than movement away from food in low-energy states.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: After 500 steps, inspect Q-table for (dist_bin=2, energy_bin=0). Assert Q for move_toward > Q for move_away.

expected_behaviors.description: Eating when already above setpoint produces negative reward (overshoot punishment).
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h_t=0.85 (above h*=0.8). Execute eat. Assert r_t < 0 (D increased due to overshoot).

references: Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2014). Homeostatic reinforcement learning for integrating reward collection and physiological stability. eLife, 3, e04811. DOI: 10.7554/eLife.04811.001

Petzschner, F.H., Garfinkel, S.N., Paulus, M.P., Koch, C., & Khalsa, S.S. (2021). Computational Models of Interoception and Body Regulation. Trends in Neurosciences, 44(1), 63-76. DOI: 10.1016/j.tins.2020.09.012
